summary: Task Prompt 9 (DEA validation on Licenses tab) fully verified. Backend: all 47 tests in tests/test_dea_validation.py pass (compute_check_digit, is_valid_dea, validate_dea_or_raise, registrant-set spot-check, matches_last_name_initial, PATCH enforcement — accept/lower-case/checksum/registrant/length/non-alnum/empty-clear/expiry-ISO/expiry-bad-format, and two audit tests). Regression: tests/test_npi_validation.py + tests/test_professional_licenses.py + tests/test_profile_self_service.py = 60 passed, 1 skipped (by design, matches the 107-in-group count once DEA is added: 47+60=107 passed, 1 skipped). Frontend on /security?tab=licenses (doctor@ccms.app): dea-card renders next to npi-card; disclaimer matches verbatim; 'ab1234563' auto-uppers to 'AB1234563', error hidden, Save enabled; 'AB1234560' shows checksum error, Save disabled; 'AB12345' shows 'exactly 9 characters'; 'IB1234563' shows registrant-type error; aria-invalid mirrors validity (true for bad, false for good), aria-describedby='dea-help'; Save with AB1234563 + 2028-12-31 → toast 'DEA saved' → persists across reload (dea_number='AB1234563', dea_expires_at='2028-12-31'). UI never claims federal DEA registration.

issues: Same HTML-maxLength paste-truncation polish as iteration_47 NPI: pasting 'AB-1234-563' (11 chars) into dea-input results in 'AB12345' (7 chars) instead of 'AB1234563' because maxLength={9} truncates to 'AB-1234-5' BEFORE the onChange strip runs (replace /[^A-Z0-9]/g then slice(0,9)). The review_request explicitly says 'Pasting AB-1234-563 strips non-alphanumerics; input enforces maxLength=9 and uppercase.' — functionally the final value still fits maxLength and is uppercased, but the user loses 2 digits. Fix: drop maxLength={9} from the Input and rely on the existing JS slice(0,9). Non-blocking; typing character-by-character works perfectly.

action_items: Optional UX polish: on /app/frontend/src/pages/account/LicensesTab.jsx DeaCard (and NpiCard — already flagged in iter_47), drop maxLength={9} from the Input so a pasted 'AB-1234-563' becomes full 'AB1234563' instead of 'AB12345'. The existing JS .replace(/[^A-Z0-9]/g,'').slice(0,9) already caps length. Non-blocking; all required flows pass.
critical_code_review_comments: core/dea.py is clean, pure-function, specific error messages per failure mode, well-documented with explicit spec + CMS worked example. Good parallel to core/npi.py.
frontend utils/dea.js mirrors backend exactly; VALID_DEA_REGISTRANT_CODES = 'ABCDEFGHJKLMPRSTUX' in both, checksum formula agrees on AB1234563 → 3.
DeaCard correctly treats empty string as 'clear the field' (valid + triggers server-side set-to-null path confirmed by test_empty_string_clears_value).

data-testids preserved (dea-card, dea-input, dea-expiry, dea-save-btn, dea-error, dea-disclaimer). Accessibility: aria-invalid flips correctly, aria-describedby='dea-help'. Disclaimer copy is verbatim — does NOT claim federal DEA registration.
Audit: profile_updated rows correctly include dea_number and dea_expires_at in metadata.fields union (both write and clear audited).

success_rate.frontend: 100% on required flows (one minor paste-truncation polish noted, same as NPI)

seed_data_creation: Backend tests create ephemeral dea_<uuid>@ccms.app doctors via admin user-create endpoint (not cleaned up — matches existing pattern in test_npi_validation.py / test_professional_licenses.py).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Task Prompt 9 is functionally complete. The paste-truncation minor issue is shared with NpiCard (iteration_47) — same root cause (HTML maxLength fires before JS strip on paste). Canonical valid DEAs: AB1234563, AB9876547, BM9999991, CF1000001. Canonical invalid (checksum): AB1234567, AB1234560. Canonical invalid registrant: IB1234563, NA1234563, QA1234563.
rca of the issue: Paste truncation (DEA + NPI): Input has both (a) JS onChange that upper-cases + strips [^A-Z0-9] + slice(0,9), and (b) HTML maxLength=9. The HTML attribute wins during browser paste because it truncates to 9 chars BEFORE firing onChange, so 'AB-1234-563' → 'AB-1234-5' → strip non-alnum → 'AB12345'. Mitigation: drop maxLength from the JSX; JS already caps length deterministically.
